title: Overturn Biden’s Offshore Energy Ban Act
short_title: Overturn Biden’s Offshore Energy Ban Act
plain_summary: This bill cancels two orders that President Biden made in January 2025 that stopped oil and gas companies from getting permits to drill underwater in federal waters. The orders covered large areas in the Gulf of Mexico, Atlantic Ocean, Pacific Ocean, and Bering Sea. By canceling these orders, the bill would allow the government to give out permits for oil and gas drilling in these ocean areas again. The original orders prevented a government agency called the Bureau of Ocean Energy Management from letting companies explore for oil and gas or drill in these underwater areas.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Overturn Biden's Offshore Energy Ban Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Canceling Presidential Orders About Offshore Areas
sections.text: The following presidential orders will have no power and will not be followed. First, the presidential order titled Memorandum on the Withdrawal of Certain Areas of the United States Outer Continental Shelf from Oil or Natural Gas Leasing, dated January sixth, two thousand twenty five. This order was about the Gulf of Mexico, Atlantic, and Pacific areas of the outer Continental Shelf (the underwater land that belongs to the United States beyond state borders). Second, the presidential order titled Memorandum on the Withdrawal of Certain Areas of the United States Outer Continental Shelf from Oil or Natural Gas Leasing, also dated January sixth, two thousand twenty five. This order was about the Bering Sea areas of the outer Continental Shelf.
